conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's good to know. Can you tell me a bit about what you do as a financial analyst in Baltimore?
Yeah, I work with numbers all day. It's not terrible, but it's not exciting either.
That sounds like a solid job. Do you have any friends or family members that you're close to?
Nah, I'm pretty alone actually. I've been focusing on my career and didn't really prioritize relationships.
Okay, that makes sense. How about your sleep lately? Have you noticed any changes?
Honestly, I've been sleeping like a rock most nights. Woke up at 3 AM the other morning and couldn't fall back asleep for hours.
That sounds frustrating. Can you tell me more about what's going on during those wakeful hours?
Just lying there feeling restless and wide awake. It's weird, I don't really remember my dreams or anything.
Have you experienced any changes in your energy levels recently?
Yeah, it feels like I've got a perpetual knot of exhaustion. Even simple tasks feel exhausting now.
That sounds tough. Are there any activities that used to bring you joy and interest that don't anymore?
Honestly, I used to love playing guitar but lately, even thinking about picking it up feels overwhelming.
Okay, let's explore that further. What is it about starting to play guitar again that feels overwhelming?
Just the thought of getting started, you know? It seems like this huge hurdle and I don't have the energy to deal with it.
That makes sense. How about your appetite lately? Has that changed at all?
Yeah, I've been eating a lot more than usual. Sometimes it's just comforting to stuff my face, you know?
Yeah, I get that. Have you noticed any changes in your mood or emotional state recently?
It feels like I'm just stuck in this perpetual funk. Nothing seems to lift me up.
Okay, let's explore what a typical day looks like for you right now. Can you walk me through it?
Yeah, I wake up late, eat something, go to work, come home and waste the rest of the day on TV or video games.
That sounds like a rough routine. Have you experienced any recent stressful life events that might be contributing to this feeling stuck?
Not really anything specific, just feeling overwhelmed by the monotony of my daily routine.
Okay, I see. It sounds like you're feeling pretty stuck right now. Is there anything that you think would help get you unstuck?
Honestly, I have no idea. Maybe if I could just shake off this exhaustion and motivation, I'd be able to tackle things again.
That makes sense. Let's work on breaking down some of those smaller tasks into more manageable chunks, sound good?
Yeah, okay. That sounds like a plan.
Okay, let me just summarize what we've talked about so far and see if there's anything you'd like to add or change before we wrap up today's session.
Yeah, actually, I did forget to mention that my appetite has been really affecting my sleep schedule. Sometimes I get up at 2 AM to eat something and then can't fall back asleep afterwards.
Okay, got it. We'll definitely want to explore that further in future sessions. Is there anything you'd like to work on or focus on in our next session?
Honestly, I just feel so lost right now. Just helping me get my life together would be amazing at this point.
Well, we can definitely work on that. Remember, everything discussed here is completely confidential and safe. You're doing great so far, keep it up!
Yeah, thanks. Thanks for listening.
Of course, take care of yourself, okay?
Will do.
Alright, see you in our next session!
